mappings.rationaleAI: International relations examining political authority at the global level, state sovereignty, and transnational dynamics addresses the politics of international relations (A). Diplomatic history and global governance connect to foreign policy and diplomacy (C).

The UN system covering the Security Council, General Assembly, and specialized agencies is a primary international organization (A.2). Peacekeeping roles address collective security through nonaggression frameworks (B.1). UN roles in dispute resolution address diplomacy in negotiation (C.2), and socio-economic development connects to foreign aid (C.3).

International law governing relations between states and organizations addresses international organizations (A.2). Treaty law including the Vienna Convention directly covers international treaties (B). Sovereignty and collective security relate to nonaggression treaties (B.1). Dispute resolution through the ICJ connects to arbitration and mediation (C.2). Treatment of armed conflict relates to international law during wartime (E.2.e).

History and practice of diplomacy as management of relations between nation-states directly addresses foreign policy and diplomacy (C). Negotiation as a universal mechanism covers diplomatic negotiation of international disputes (C.2). The Treaty of Versailles and wartime summitry relate to treaties terminating wars (B.2).

Peacemaking, peacebuilding, and conflict resolution frameworks address collective security (B.1). UN Security Council peacekeeping operations relate to diplomacy in negotiating disputes (C.2). Just war theory and structural conditions for violence connect to war among states (E).

Roles of NATO, EU, and UN address international organizations (A.2). Nuclear weapons proliferation relates to arms limitation (B.3). State versus human security frameworks and the politics of fear connect to intelligence activities (D). War, peace, and terrorism address war among states (E) and degrees of war (E.1).

Superpower rivalry between the US and Soviet Union addresses the politics of international relations (A). NATO formation relates to collective security (B.1).
